steps.title: Identify what became uncertain
steps.content: The passage says Bellamy claimed to have written the pledge in August 1892. A historian then found a newspaper article from several months earlier describing students reciting the same pledge. The passage concludes: "Bellamy may not have created the pledge after all." What was once accepted as fact — that Bellamy was the original author — is now in doubt.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "How many changes were made to the pledge in the 1900s.": The passage never mentions changes made after 1892. ✗
  • "Which part of the pledge students liked best in 1892.": Student preferences are never discussed in the passage. ✗
  • "The name of the newspaper that first published the pledge.": The passage mentions a newspaper article was discovered but does not raise doubt about the newspaper's name. ✗
  • "The identity of the person who first wrote the pledge.": The new evidence directly calls into question whether Bellamy was truly the original author — exactly what became uncertain. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
